diagrams.title: Partial Derivative Visualization on a Surface
diagrams.description: A 3D surface plot of a function f(x,y) with a highlighted point and two tangent lines showing the partial derivatives ∂f/∂x (movement along x-axis) and ∂f/∂y (movement along y-axis). The x-y plane shows the grid with the z-axis rising vertically.
diagrams.purpose: Geometrically illustrates what partial derivatives represent - the rate of change of a function in the direction of each coordinate axis while holding other variables constant

diagrams.title: Del Operator (Nabla) Components
diagrams.description: A clear vector notation display showing the Del operator ∇ in 3D Cartesian coordinates. Shows the component breakdown: ∇ = i(∂/∂x) + j(∂/∂y) + k(∂/∂z), with unit vectors i, j, k pointing along x, y, z axes and corresponding partial derivative operators.
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the Del operator's structure and components, showing students that ∇ combines a vector of direction with the partial derivative operation

diagrams.title: Three Applications of Del Operator
diagrams.description: Three separate visualizations showing gradient, divergence, and curl operations. Gradient: shows scalar field with arrows pointing outward/upward (mountains). Divergence: shows vector field with arrows pointing outward from a point (source) or inward (sink). Curl: shows rotating circular arrows indicating rotation.
elements: gradient diagram with upward arrows on contour map
divergence diagram with outward-pointing arrows from center
curl diagram with circular rotation arrows
formulas: ∇f for gradient, ∇·F for divergence, ∇×F for curl
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the three fundamental operations performed with the Del operator - gradient (produces vector from scalar), divergence (produces scalar from vector), and curl (produces vector from vector)
